generator_prompt_injection:
REAL TEST-TAKER INSIGHTS (sourced from r/ACT, updated 2026-06-10):
- Coverage: Real test-takers report this exam covers breadth over depth. Stay at college 101 level on each topic.
- High-emphasis topics (per real students): math, english.
- Students struggle most with: reading. Generate proportionally more on these.
- Wording style: ~0% of items should use realistic exam-style indirection (NOT unfair ambiguity).
- Difficulty target: 30% easy / 50% medium / 20% hard. avg pass score 29 suggests easier test.
- Authenticity bar: students compare us to ACT class, practice tests. Match that register.

popup_tips.text: On ACT READING, real test-takers say breadth beats depth. Aim to recognize many topics at a 101 level rather than mastering a few in deep detail.
popup_tips.source_attribution: Compiled from real test-takers on r/ACT

popup_tips.text: Heaviest-weighted topics on the actual ACT READING exam (per recent test-takers): math, english. Spend more time here.
popup_tips.source_attribution: Compiled from real test-takers on r/ACT

popup_tips.text: Where ACT READING students reported struggling most: reading. Make sure you can handle these before exam day.
popup_tips.source_attribution: Compiled from real test-takers on r/ACT

popup_tips.text: Resources real ACT READING test-takers credited: ACT class and practice tests. Pair with practice questions.
popup_tips.source_attribution: Compiled from real test-takers on r/ACT

popup_tips.text: A passing score on ACT READING is reachable: recent successful test-takers reported scoring around 29 after focused study.
popup_tips.source_attribution: Compiled from real test-takers on r/ACT
